title: Data access and caching
text: Live downloads cached JSON user cache directory. can inspect cache location size:
code: cache_dir <- tools::R_user_dir("vpdsus", which = "cache") cache_files <- if (dir.exists(cache_dir)) list.files(cache_dir, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE) else character() tibble::tibble(   cache_dir = cache_dir,   n_files = length(cache_files),   bytes = if (length(cache_files)) sum(file.info(cache_files)$size, na.rm = TRUE) else 0 ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   cache_dir                    n_files bytes #>   <chr>                          <int> <dbl> #> 1 /home/runner/.cache/R/vpdsus       0     0

title: Data access and caching
text: GHO API exposes indicator catalogue. Use gho_find_indicator() search code need, pass code get_coverage() get_cases(). vignette uses pinned fixture can run deterministically CI:
code: # Search the WHO GHO indicator metadata (offline fixture for this vignette) m <- gho_find_indicator("measles", top_n = 10, offline = TRUE) print(m) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   indicator_code indicator_name                                                  #>   <chr>          <chr>                                                           #> 1 MCV2           Measles-containing-vaccine second-dose (MCV2) immunization cov… #> 2 mslv           Measles immunization coverage among one-year-olds (%)           #> 3 vmsl           Measles immunization coverage among one-year-olds (%)           #> 4 WHS2_168       Distribution of causes of death among children aged <5 years (… #> 5 WHS3_62        Measles - number of reported cases                              #> 6 WHS8_110       Measles-containing-vaccine first-dose (MCV1) immunization cove…  # Two useful measles-related indicators in this list are: # - WHS8_110: MCV1 coverage # - WHS3_62: reported measles cases

title: Data access and caching
text: keep vignette deterministic, use pinned single-country fixtures : MCV1 coverage (USA, 2020) via indicator code WHS8_110 measles cases (USA, 2020) via indicator code WHS3_62 corresponding live calls : read pinned raw JSON standardise package output columns.
code: get_coverage("MCV1") get_cases("measles") # Coverage fixture p_cov <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "gho_WHS8_110_USA_2020.json", package = "vpdsus") txt_cov <- paste(readLines(p_cov, warn = FALSE, encoding = "UTF-8"), collapse = "\n") raw_cov <- jsonlite::fromJSON(txt_cov)$value cov <- tibble::as_tibble(raw_cov) |>   dplyr::transmute(     iso3 = toupper(trimws(as.character(.data$SpatialDim))),     year = as.integer(.data$TimeDim),     coverage = as.numeric(.data$Value) / 100   )  cov #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   iso3   year coverage #>   <chr> <int>    <dbl> #> 1 USA    2020     0.91  # Cases fixture p_cases <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "gho_WHS3_62_USA_2020.json", package = "vpdsus") txt_cases <- paste(readLines(p_cases, warn = FALSE, encoding = "UTF-8"), collapse = "\n") raw_cases <- jsonlite::fromJSON(txt_cases)$value cases <- tibble::as_tibble(raw_cases) |>   dplyr::transmute(     iso3 = toupper(trimws(as.character(.data$SpatialDim))),     year = as.integer(.data$TimeDim),     cases = as.numeric(.data$Value)   )  cases #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   iso3   year cases #>   <chr> <int> <dbl> #> 1 USA    2020  1275

title: Data access and caching
text: standardised coverage, cases, demography series can join country-year panel downstream modelling:
code: demog <- get_demography(source = "fixture_wpp", years = 2020, countries = "USA")  panel_usa_2020 <- build_panel(   coverage = cov,   cases = cases,   demography = demog )  panel_usa_2020 #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   iso3   year pop_total  pop_0_4 pop_5_14  births coverage cases #>   <chr> <int>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 USA    2020  62250000 19500000 42750000 3700000     0.91  1275 panel_diagnostics(panel_usa_2020) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>       n missing_coverage missing_cases missing_pop #>   <int>            <int>         <int>       <int> #> 1     1                0             0           0

title: Data access and caching
text: functions focus data retrieval standardisation. typical end--end analysis : builds modelling-ready panel (coverage + cases + demography), estimates susceptibility, produces -style risk ranking (optionally fits outbreak models). repository ships vpdsus_example_panel() global measles panel workflow can demonstrated realistic scale without live downloads:   See vignettes method choices modelling: Susceptibility estimates -style ranking plots Outbreak models
code: panel <- vpdsus_example_panel()  # 1) Susceptibility estimation (simple static method) sus <- estimate_susceptible_static(panel, coverage_col = "coverage", pop_col = "pop_0_4")  # 2) Risk ranking (summarise recent coverage and take susceptibility at a year) r <- risk_rank(panel, sus, window_years = 3, year_end = 2020)  # 3) Inspect / plot head(r) #> # A tibble: 6 × 9 #>   iso3  coverage_mean who_region country   susceptible_n susceptible_prop method #>   <chr>         <dbl> <chr>      <chr>             <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>  #> 1 IND           0.923 SAS        India         13549246.             0.11 static #> 2 NGA           0.58  SSF        Nigeria       12219392.             0.4  static #> 3 ETH           0.557 SSF        Ethiopia       6894777.             0.43 static #> 4 IDN           0.843 EAS        Indonesia      5679747.             0.24 static #> 5 COD           0.637 SSF        Congo, D…      5599412.             0.38 static #> 6 PAK           0.81  MEA        Pakistan       5041704.             0.17 static #> # ℹ 2 more variables: risk_category <fct>, rank_global <int> plot_coverage_rank(r, top_n = 5) plot_susceptible_rank(r, top_n = 5)

title: Mechanistic modelling in odin2 (annual SIRV)
text: Model choices package: balance_discrete: one-state susceptible recurrence (teaching bridge Method D). sirv_annual: annual S, , R, V model births, vaccination, infection, recovery, mortality, reported cases output.
code: library(vpdsus)

title: Mechanistic modelling in odin2 (annual SIRV)
text: Start one-state difference equation model: S[t+1] = S[t] + inc Interpretation: inc = 1, state increases linearly step.
code: mdl <- odin2_build_model("minimal_discrete") out <- odin2_simulate(   mdl,   times = 0:10,   pars = list(inc = 1),   initial = list(S = 0) )  out #> # A tibble: 11 × 2 #>     time     S #>    <int> <dbl> #>  1     0     0 #>  2     1     1 #>  3     2     2 #>  4     3     3 #>  5     4     4 #>  6     5     5 #>  7     6     6 #>  8     7     7 #>  9     8     8 #> 10     9     9 #> 11    10    10

title: Mechanistic modelling in odin2 (annual SIRV)
text: balance_discrete model uses: S[t+1] = max(0, S[t] + births[t] - vaccinated[t] - cases[t] / rho) parameters, susceptibles decrease 1 step floor 0.
code: mdl_bal <- odin2_build_model("balance_discrete") out_bal <- odin2_simulate(   mdl_bal,   times = 0:5,   pars = list(rho = 1, births = 0, vaccinated = 0, cases = 1),   initial = list(S = 3) )  out_bal #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>    time     S #>   <int> <dbl> #> 1     0     3 #> 2     1     2 #> 3     2     1 #> 4     3     0 #> 5     4     0 #> 6     5     0

title: Mechanistic modelling in odin2 (annual SIRV)
code: times <- 0:4 out_tv <- odin2_simulate(   mdl_bal,   times = times,   pars = list(rho = 1),   initial = list(S = 3),   inputs = list(     births = c(0, 1, 0, 0, 0),     vaccinated = 0,     cases = c(0, 0, 1, 0, 2)   ) )  out_tv #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>    time     S #>   <int> <dbl> #> 1     0     3 #> 2     1     4 #> 3     2     3 #> 4     3     3 #> 5     4     1

title: Mechanistic modelling in odin2 (annual SIRV)
Mechanistic modelling in odin2 (annual SIRV)
text: balance_discrete intended match Method D matched assumptions.
code: sus_d <- estimate_susceptible_case_balance(   panel_ok |> dplyr::filter(.data$iso3 == .env$iso3),   rho = 1,   s0 = 0,   coverage_col = "coverage",   births_col = "births",   cases_col = "cases",   pop_col = "pop_total",   age_group = "total" )  cmp <- dplyr::left_join(   dplyr::mutate(out_panel, year = time),   dplyr::select(sus_d, year, susceptible_n),   by = "year" )  cmp #> # A tibble: 24 × 4 #>     time        S  year susceptible_n #>    <int>    <dbl> <int>         <dbl> #>  1  2000       0   2000            0  #>  2  2001  625376.  2001       750135. #>  3  2002 1282773.  2002      1375511. #>  4  2003 1934508.  2003      2032908. #>  5  2004 2503640.  2004      2684643. #>  6  2005 3050861.  2005      3253776. #>  7  2006 3575681.  2006      3800997. #>  8  2007 4085585.  2007      4325817. #>  9  2008 4545858.  2008      4835720. #> 10  2009 5006207.  2009      5295993. #> # ℹ 14 more rows ggplot2::ggplot(cmp, ggplot2::aes(x = year)) +   ggplot2::geom_line(ggplot2::aes(y = S, colour = "odin2"), linewidth = 1) +   ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(y = S, colour = "odin2"), size = 2) +   ggplot2::geom_line(ggplot2::aes(y = susceptible_n, colour = "method_d"), linewidth = 1, linetype = 2) +   ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(y = susceptible_n, colour = "method_d"), size = 2, shape = 1) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c(odin2 = "#1b9e77", method_d = "#d95f02")) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Year",     y = "Susceptible (count)",     colour = NULL,     title = paste0("Susceptible balance: odin2 vs Method D (", iso3, ")")   ) +   ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

title: Mechanistic modelling in odin2 (annual SIRV)
text: Calibration question: rho value make model match chosen susceptible count target year?
code: target_year <- max(times) target_susceptible_n <- max(1, out_panel$S[[length(out_panel$S)]] * 0.9)  rho_hat <- tryCatch(   calibrate_rho_case_balance(     panel_ok,     iso3 = iso3,     years = times,     target_year = target_year,     target_susceptible_n = target_susceptible_n,     rho_interval = c(0.01, 1)   ),   error = function(e) {     message("Calibration failed for this panel slice; using rho = 1 for demonstration.")     1   } ) #> Calibration failed for this panel slice; using rho = 1 for demonstration.  rho_hat #> [1] 1 out_cal <- odin2_simulate(   mdl_bal,   times = times,   pars = list(rho = rho_hat),   initial = list(S = 0),   inputs = inputs )  out_cal #> # A tibble: 24 × 2 #>     time        S #>    <int>    <dbl> #>  1  2000       0  #>  2  2001  625376. #>  3  2002 1282773. #>  4  2003 1934508. #>  5  2004 2503640. #>  6  2005 3050861. #>  7  2006 3575681. #>  8  2007 4085585. #>  9  2008 4545858. #> 10  2009 5006207. #> # ℹ 14 more rows

title: Mechanistic modelling in odin2 (annual SIRV)
text: annual SIRV model includes: compartments S, , R, V, births S, vaccination flow S -> V, infection S -> , recovery -> R, mortality compartments, reporting fraction rho applied infections. Key outputs inspect susceptibility inference: reported_cases: model-implied observed burden, susceptible_n: susceptible count, susceptible_prop: susceptible proportion.
code: mdl_sirv <- odin2_build_model("sirv_annual") times <- 0:15  out_sirv <- odin2_simulate(   mdl_sirv,   times = times,   pars = list(     beta = 0.8,     gamma = 0.4,     mu = 0.01,     rho = 0.25,     births = 50000,     vaccinated = 40000,     n_pop = 1e7   ),   initial = list(     S = 2e6,     I = 1e3,     R = 7.95e6,     V = 5e4   ) )  head(out_sirv) #> # A tibble: 6 × 8 #>    time      S     I      R      V reported_cases susceptible_n susceptible_prop #>   <int>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>         <dbl>            <dbl> #> 1     0 2   e6 1000  7.95e6 5   e4            0              0             0     #> 2     1 1.99e6  750  7.87e6 8.95e4           40        2000000             0.2   #> 3     2 1.98e6  562. 7.79e6 1.29e5           29.8      1989840             0.199 #> 4     3 1.97e6  421. 7.71e6 1.67e5           22.2      1979822.            0.198 #> 5     4 1.96e6  314. 7.64e6 2.06e5           16.6      1969935.            0.197 #> 6     5 1.95e6  235. 7.56e6 2.44e5           12.3      1960169.            0.196 dplyr::select(out_sirv, time, reported_cases, susceptible_n, susceptible_prop) |>   head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>    time reported_cases susceptible_n susceptible_prop #>   <int>          <dbl>         <dbl>            <dbl> #> 1     0            0              0             0     #> 2     1           40        2000000             0.2   #> 3     2           29.8      1989840             0.199 #> 4     3           22.2      1979822.            0.198 #> 5     4           16.6      1969935.            0.197 #> 6     5           12.3      1960169.            0.196

title: Outbreak modelling
text: Interpretation guide: estimate > 0 susceptible_prop means higher susceptibility associated higher outbreak odds. region/year terms absorb broad structural differences temporal trend.
code: cmp_df <- mod_pc |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(outcome), !is.na(coverage), !is.na(susceptible_prop))  fit_cov <- stats::glm(outcome ~ coverage + year + who_region, data = cmp_df, family = stats::binomial()) fit_sus <- stats::glm(outcome ~ susceptible_prop + year + who_region, data = cmp_df, family = stats::binomial()) fit_both <- stats::glm(outcome ~ coverage + susceptible_prop + year + who_region, data = cmp_df, family = stats::binomial())  data.frame(   model = c("coverage_only", "susceptibility_only", "coverage_plus_susceptibility"),   aic = c(AIC(fit_cov), AIC(fit_sus), AIC(fit_both)) ) #>                          model      aic #> 1                coverage_only 2471.869 #> 2          susceptibility_only 2471.869 #> 3 coverage_plus_susceptibility 2471.869

title: Outbreak modelling
text: Use [evaluate_outbreak_time_split()] : train/test split time, summary metrics, prediction calibration plots. Metrics meaning: accuracy: classification performance threshold 0.5 brier: probability calibration/discrimination loss (lower better) log_loss: penalises overconfident errors auc: threshold-free discrimination Inspect test-set predictions: Diagnostic plots:
code: ev <- evaluate_outbreak_time_split(mod_pc) ev$metrics #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   train_end n_train n_test prevalence accuracy  brier log_loss   auc #>       <dbl>   <int>  <int>      <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1      2015    2875   1414      0.103    0.897 0.0958    0.406 0.724 head(dplyr::select(ev$predictions, iso3, year, year_next, outcome, prob_outbreak, pred_outbreak)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   iso3   year year_next outcome prob_outbreak pred_outbreak #>   <chr> <dbl>     <dbl>   <int>         <dbl>         <int> #> 1 AFG    2016      2017       0        0.0589             0 #> 2 AFG    2017      2018       0        0.0518             0 #> 3 AFG    2018      2019       0        0.0427             0 #> 4 AFG    2019      2020       0        0.0495             0 #> 5 AFG    2020      2021       0        0.0435             0 #> 6 AFG    2021      2022       1        0.0459             0 ev$plots$time ev$plots$calibration

text: Function: [estimate_susceptible_static()] Core idea: susceptible proportion approximately 1 - (coverage * VE). susceptible count proportion multiplied population denominator. vignette use: coverage immunisation proportion, pop_0_4 denominator simple child-age signal, VE = 1 (default) minimal didactic example.
code: sA <- estimate_susceptible_static(   panel,   coverage_col = "coverage",   pop_col = "pop_0_4" )  head(dplyr::select(sA, iso3, year, susceptible_prop, susceptible_n, method)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   iso3   year susceptible_prop susceptible_n method #>   <chr> <int>            <dbl>         <dbl> <chr>  #> 1 ABW    1960               NA            NA static #> 2 ABW    1961               NA            NA static #> 3 ABW    1962               NA            NA static #> 4 ABW    1963               NA            NA static #> 5 ABW    1964               NA            NA static #> 6 ABW    1965               NA            NA static

what: Method B: cohort reconstruction from births and coverage
title: Susceptibility estimates and WHO-style ranking plots
text: Function: [estimate_susceptible_cohort()] Core idea: reconstruct recent birth cohorts make age window (default 0-4), cohort, count births effectively immunised, aggregate across window divide age-group population. Equation year y years_back = 4: S_n(y) = sum_{k=0..4} births(y-k) * (1 - coverage(y-k)) S_prop(y) = S_n(y) / pop_0_4(y) Method B explicitly answers: “many children currently aged 0-4 likely remained susceptible, given birth cohort sizes coverage observed cohort vaccinated?”

what: Method B walkthrough (single country, step by step)
title: Susceptibility estimates and WHO-style ranking plots
text: make logic concrete, take one country one year rebuild cohort sum hand. Manual Method B numerator example year: Compare manual reconstruction package output: large global panel method often much informative Method , uses cohort size birth-history dynamics directly rather current coverage.
code: demo_candidates <- panel |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(coverage), !is.na(births), !is.na(pop_0_4)) |>   dplyr::count(iso3, name = "n_years") |>   dplyr::filter(n_years >= 5) |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n_years))  iso_demo <- demo_candidates$iso3[[1]] year_demo <- panel |>   dplyr::filter(iso3 == iso_demo, !is.na(coverage), !is.na(births), !is.na(pop_0_4)) |>   dplyr::summarise(year_demo = max(year)) |>   dplyr::pull(year_demo)  cohort_tbl <- panel |>   dplyr::filter(iso3 == iso_demo, year %in% (year_demo - 4):year_demo) |>   dplyr::arrange(year) |>   dplyr::transmute(     iso3,     year,     births,     coverage,     susceptible_from_cohort = births * (1 - coverage)   )  cohort_tbl #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   iso3   year   births coverage susceptible_from_cohort #>   <chr> <int>    <dbl>    <dbl>                   <dbl> #> 1 AFG    2019 1405901.     0.57                 604537. #> 2 AFG    2020 1429964.     0.57                 614884. #> 3 AFG    2021 1453695.     0.51                 712311. #> 4 AFG    2022 1462664.     0.56                 643572. #> 5 AFG    2023 1469032.     0.55                 661065. manual_s_n <- sum(cohort_tbl$susceptible_from_cohort, na.rm = TRUE) manual_s_n #> [1] 3236369 sB <- estimate_susceptible_cohort(   panel,   coverage_col = "coverage",   births_col = "births",   pop_col = "pop_0_4" )  head(dplyr::select(sB, iso3, year, susceptible_prop, susceptible_n, method)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   iso3   year susceptible_prop susceptible_n method #>   <chr> <int>            <dbl>         <dbl> <chr>  #> 1 ABW    1960                0             0 cohort #> 2 ABW    1961                0             0 cohort #> 3 ABW    1962                0             0 cohort #> 4 ABW    1963                0             0 cohort #> 5 ABW    1964                0             0 cohort #> 6 ABW    1965                0             0 cohort sB_demo <- sB |>   dplyr::filter(iso3 == iso_demo, year == year_demo) |>   dplyr::select(iso3, year, susceptible_n, susceptible_prop)  manual_check <- panel |>   dplyr::filter(iso3 == iso_demo, year == year_demo) |>   dplyr::transmute(     iso3,     year,     pop_0_4,     manual_susceptible_n = manual_s_n,     manual_susceptible_prop = manual_s_n / pop_0_4   ) |>   dplyr::left_join(sB_demo, by = c("iso3", "year"))  manual_check #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   iso3   year  pop_0_4 manual_susceptible_n manual_susceptible_p…¹ susceptible_n #>   <chr> <int>    <dbl>                <dbl>                  <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 AFG    2023 5973106.             3236369.                  0.542      3236369. #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​manual_susceptible_prop #> # ℹ 1 more variable: susceptible_prop <dbl>

what: Method D: case-balance recurrence with reporting fraction rho
title: Susceptibility estimates and WHO-style ranking plots
text: Function: [estimate_susceptible_case_balance()] Core recurrence (discrete-time): S[t+1] = max(0, S[t] + births[t] - vaccinated[t] - cases[t] / rho) Interpretation: lower rho means -reporting therefore larger implied depletion infection observed cases. Choosing method first project: Start Method understand workflow quickly. Move Method B births cohort structure matter. Use Method D reported cases considered informative depletion.
code: sD <- estimate_susceptible_case_balance(   panel,   rho = c(0.2, 0.5, 1),   coverage_col = "coverage",   births_col = "births",   cases_col = "cases",   pop_col = "pop_total",   age_group = "total" )  head(dplyr::select(sD, iso3, year, rho, susceptible_prop, susceptible_n, method)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   iso3   year   rho susceptible_prop susceptible_n method       #>   <chr> <int> <dbl>            <dbl>         <dbl> <chr>        #> 1 ABW    1960   0.2           0.0320         1760. case_balance #> 2 ABW    1961   0.2          NA                NA  case_balance #> 3 ABW    1962   0.2          NA                NA  case_balance #> 4 ABW    1963   0.2          NA                NA  case_balance #> 5 ABW    1964   0.2          NA                NA  case_balance #> 6 ABW    1965   0.2          NA                NA  case_balance

title: Susceptibility estimates and WHO-style ranking plots
text: Method easiest baseline useful quick screening. Method B adds births/cohort structure usually epidemiologically interpretable child susceptibility. Method D introduces case-driven depletion reporting parameter (rho), bridging towards mechanistic inference.
